meta.title: Baltic Adventure 2026
meta.subtitle: Michael & Grace's Complete Travel Guide
meta.theme: Hidden rooms, medieval streets, grand hotels, candlelit cellars, castles, and Baltic countryside
meta.currency_note: All four countries use the euro (€). USD figures are approximate.

flights.notes: Departure day. Leave a comfortable airport buffer.
Arrive Helsinki 4:40 PM — taxi or tram to Hotel Lilla Roberts, easy night.
Very early departure — target airport arrival ~5:30 AM.
Home directly from the Vilnius routing — no Helsinki backtrack.

ticket.open_item: Verify Grace has a SEPARATELY ISSUED ticket — the uploaded e-ticket lists only Michael.

hotels.name: Hotel Lilla Roberts
hotels.why: Arrival night and recovery before the ferry to Tallinn. A jewel-box Art Deco hotel in the Design District — exactly the kind of room you like to step into tired and feel held.
hotels.nearby_coffee: Hotel's own Krog Roba bar & restaurant; cafés along Iso Roobertinkatu pedestrian street.
hotels.nearby_stumble: Design District lanes, Esplanadi park, Market Square & Old Market Hall (Vanha Kauppahalli), Diana Park.
hotels.essentials: Pharmacy (Apteekki) and groceries (K-Market / Alepa) within a few blocks on Iso Roobertinkatu; ATMs (Otto.) nearby.
hotels.notes: One night only — don't overplan. Drop bags, shower, wander to dinner.

hotels.name: Hotel Telegraaf, Autograph Collection
hotels.why: Your main 'stumble' base inside Tallinn's medieval core — a polished historic hotel on Vene Street, steps from St. Catherine's Passage. (The alternative once considered was the Schlössle Hotel, whose fireplace lounge is still worth a non-guest visit.)
hotels.nearby_coffee: Café Maiasmokk (oldest café in Tallinn, ~3 min walk); Kehrwieder on Town Hall Square.
hotels.nearby_stumble: Vene Street, St. Catherine's Passage, Pikk Street, Town Hall Square — all on the doorstep.
hotels.essentials: Pharmacies (Apteek) and Rimi/Coop groceries around Viru and Town Hall Square; ATMs throughout Old Town.
hotels.notes: Old Town location means cobblestones — pack comfortable shoes. Telegraaf's own bar is a fine nightcap room.

hotels.name: Grand Palace Hotel
hotels.why: Riga Old Town at its most refined — an old-world grand hotel a short walk from the Art Nouveau district and the city's candlelit cellars. The peak 'unplanned jaw-dropping room' city, and this is the room to come home to.
hotels.purpose: Riga Old Town / Art Nouveau / cellar base (3 nights)
hotels.nearby_coffee: Hotel's own Orangerie / bar; cafés around Doma laukums (Dome Square) a few minutes away.
hotels.nearby_stumble: Old Town lanes toward the Dome Cathedral and House of the Black Heads; Art Nouveau on Alberta & Elizabetes iela ~15 min walk.
hotels.essentials: Pharmacies (Aptieka — Mēness aptieka) and Rimi groceries nearby; ATMs around Old Town.
hotels.notes: Verify parking before car pickup on Jul 16 — Old Town access is restricted; ask the hotel about its garage or a nearby lot.

hotels.name: Hotel PACAI
hotels.why: A 17th-century palazzo turned design hotel on Didžioji Street, with a hidden courtyard and a vaulted feel. Vilnius magic hides behind archways, and PACAI is one of them.
hotels.nearby_coffee: Hotel's own Nineteen18 restaurant/bar; cafés along Pilies and Didžioji streets.
hotels.nearby_stumble: Pilies Street spine, Town Hall Square, courtyards off Didžioji, and Užupis just across the river.
hotels.essentials: Pharmacies (Vaistinė) and groceries (IKI / Rimi) around the Old Town; ATMs nearby.
hotels.notes: OPEN ITEM: screenshot showed '1 Adult.' Confirm the booking is for 2 adults / Grace included before arrival.

car.dropoff_should_be: Jul 19 very early at VNO (before the 7:50 AM flight) OR Jul 18 evening at VNO + taxi to airport Jul 19.
car.cost_estimate: Not shown on screenshot; Michael estimated ≈ USD 500–600.
car.status: Confirmed — return date WRONG, must fix
open_items: URGENT: Return date currently Jul 21 — flight departs Jul 19 at 7:50 AM. Change to Jul 19 early, or return Jul 18 evening.
Confirm cross-border driving Latvia → Lithuania is explicitly allowed/included.
Confirm one-way drop (RIX pickup → VNO return) is included in the quote.
Check insurance deductible and whether a credit-card CDW applies in Latvia & Lithuania.
Confirm after-hours / early-morning return procedure at VNO.

open_items.text: Verify Grace has a separately issued flight ticket (e-ticket lists only Michael).
Correct rental car return: change Jul 21 → Jul 19 (early) or Jul 18 evening at VNO.
Confirm Hotel PACAI is booked for 2 adults, not 1 adult.
Book Helsinki → Tallinn ferry (Tallink) for Jul 10, morning/midday.
Book Tallinn → Riga transport (Lux Express bus) for Jul 14.
Confirm car rental includes cross-border Latvia→Lithuania and one-way drop-off.
Confirm after-hours / early return procedure at Vilnius Airport (VNO).
Check parking near Grand Palace Riga and Hotel PACAI Vilnius.
Download offline Google Maps for Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania.
Add all bookings to the calendar.
Confirm automatic transmission in writing on the car booking.
Check insurance deductible / credit-card CDW coverage for LV & LT.

days.theme: Departure day. The trip begins the moment you leave for the airport.
days.morning: Final packing and the before-departure checklist. Double-check passports, the Air France booking ref (XLOIRG), and that bags are within 23 kg.
days.afternoon: Depart Salt Lake City 3:35 PM. Settle in for the long leg to Paris.
days.evening: Sleep on the plane if you can — Helsinki is +9 hours and you land tomorrow afternoon.
days.reservation: Flight ticket XLOIRG · 1 × 23 kg checked bag each.
days.food: Eat a real meal at SLC before boarding; airline food is a bonus, not a plan.
days.backup: If the SLC→CDG leg slips, the CDG→HEL connection (12:35 PM Jul 9) has a buffer, but know your rebooking options.

days.transport: Paris → Helsinki (AF1070, arr 4:40 PM); taxi or tram to hotel
days.theme: Land softly. Recover. No hard scheduling tonight.
days.morning: In transit — Paris CDG connection, depart 12:35 PM. Seat 15A on the Helsinki leg.
days.afternoon: Arrive Helsinki 4:40 PM. Taxi (~30 min) or the train + tram to Lilla Roberts. Check in, shower, reset.
days.evening: Easy harbor-side wander and an unhurried dinner near the hotel. You've been traveling 24 hours — let it be gentle.
days.reservation: Hotel Lilla Roberts — 1 night, EUR 147.90. I Prefer #PHWABARVT3.
days.stumble: Drift down Iso Roobertinkatu, cut to the Esplanadi promenade, and look in at the Old Market Hall (Vanha Kauppahalli) if it's still open. Don't force it — it's a stretch-your-legs night.
days.food: Dinner near the hotel: Savotta (rustic Finnish, by the cathedral) or the hotel's own Krog Roba. Keep it simple.
days.backup: Rain: Esplanadi cafés, the Old Market Hall, or just the hotel bar. Jet-lagged is allowed.

days.theme: Cross the gulf and step into the medieval core.
days.morning: Relaxed checkout from Lilla Roberts. Coffee, maybe a last look at Market Square, then to the West Terminal for the ferry.
days.afternoon: Tallink ferry to Tallinn (~2 hours). Arrive, walk or short taxi to Hotel Telegraaf on Vene Street, check into your 4-night home.
days.evening: First Old Town drift: Town Hall Square, Pikk Street, Vene Street. Let the medieval streets do the work.
days.reservation: Hotel Telegraaf — 4 nights, conf. 93263159. BOOK the Tallink ferry in advance.
days.stumble: From the terminal you'll come up into the Old Town walls — follow the lanes that look oldest. St. Catherine's Passage at dusk is the first 'oh' moment.
days.food: Dinner in Old Town — Rataskaevu 16 (book ahead) or Lore Bistroo. A nightcap in the Telegraaf bar.
days.backup: Rough water or rain: ferries run regardless; just stay covered between terminal and hotel.

days.theme: The pure Tallinn stumble day. This is what you came for.
days.morning: Start at Maiasmokk for coffee and marzipan, then wander Vene and Pikk with no agenda.
days.afternoon: Hidden courtyards, cellar cafés, the Master's Courtyard (Meistrite Hoov). Duck into Pierre Chocolaterie. Let one lane lead to the next.
days.evening: St. Catherine's Passage after dark; a fireplace-lounge moment — the Schlössle Hotel lounge as a non-guest, or the Telegraaf bar.
days.stumble: TODAY IS THE STUMBLE. No checklist — follow the cobblestones, the smell of coffee, the open door. The 'best hidden room' of the trip is probably found today.
days.food: Café Maiasmokk, Kehrwieder (Saiakang lane), Kofeman, Loulou for breakfast. Dinner: Rataskaevu 16 if not done.
days.backup: Rain makes the cellar cafés better, not worse. This is a rain-proof day.

days.theme: Walls, towers, and viewpoints — with Medieval Days as ambient festival.
days.morning: Up to Toompea for the Patkuli and Kohtuotsa viewpoints over the red roofs. Danish King's Garden and the towers along the way.
days.afternoon: Kiek in de Kök and the town wall walk. Medieval Days (Jul 10–12) may be winding through the streets — treat it as atmosphere, not a destination.
days.evening: Back into St. Catherine's Passage after dark; dinner leaning medieval or modern, your mood.
days.reservation: Keep flexible. Olde Hansa needs no booking for a drink; Rataskaevu 16 does for dinner.
days.stumble: Medieval Days means costumed lanes and market stalls appearing where they weren't yesterday — wander into it rather than chasing a program.
days.food: Dinner: Rataskaevu 16 (modern Estonian) or Olde Hansa (full medieval theatre) if you want to lean in.
days.backup: Rain: the towers and Kiek in de Kök are largely indoors; the wall walk can wait.

days.transport: On foot, tram, or a short taxi
days.theme: Contrast day — industrial-creative Tallinn outside the walls.
days.morning: Telliskivi Creative City and Kalamaja's wooden houses — coffee, design shops, street art. A different texture entirely.
days.afternoon: Either the Seaplane Harbour (Lennusadam) maritime museum or Kadriorg Park & Palace and the Fotografiska rooftop.
days.evening: Back to Old Town for a last quiet night before Riga. Easy dinner.
days.reservation: Optional dinner reservation if you want one — otherwise loose.
days.stumble: Kalamaja's back streets are the stumble zone here — wooden facades, tiny galleries, the Balti Jaam market.
days.food: Lunch/coffee in Telliskivi; Fotografiska rooftop for a drink; Lore Bistroo or a Kalamaja spot for dinner.
days.backup: Rain: Seaplane Harbour and Fotografiska are indoor anchors.

days.transport: Lux Express bus Tallinn → Riga (~4.5 hrs)
days.theme: Move south. Arrive into Riga's Old Town in the evening.
days.morning: Checkout from Telegraaf. Coffee, last Old Town look, to the bus station (Tallinn Bus Station / Autobussijaam).
days.afternoon: Lux Express to Riga (~4.5 hrs, comfortable coaches with wifi). Arrive, taxi to Grand Palace Hotel, settle in.
days.evening: Loose Old Town drift. Riga Black Magic for the riding-bar-and-balsam ritual, the Grand Palace bar, an easy dinner.
days.reservation: Grand Palace Hotel — 3 nights, conf. 48469SG014613. BOOK Lux Express in advance.
days.stumble: Riga reveals itself slowly from the bus station toward Old Town — let the first cellar door you pass pull you in.
days.food: Riga Black Magic (atmosphere + Riga Black Balsam), Grand Palace bar, or a relaxed Old Town dinner. Keep it loose.
days.backup: If the bus is delayed, dinner plans flex — Old Town kitchens run late.

days.theme: The jaw-dropping stumble day. Riga is the peak unplanned-room city.
days.morning: Art Nouveau morning: Alberta iela and Elizabetes iela — the most extravagant facades in Europe. Coffee at Art Café Sienna among the period rooms.
days.afternoon: Back to Old Town for the cellars: Riga Black Magic, the House of the Black Heads exterior, hidden vaulted bars.
days.evening: Rozengrāls — the candlelit medieval cellar — is the one worth reserving. Everything else stays loose.
days.reservation: RECOMMENDATION: reserve Rozengrāls for dinner. Leave the rest of the day unplanned.
days.stumble: This is Riga's day to ambush you with beauty. Wander between the Art Nouveau district and the Old Town cellars and let the rooms find you.
days.food: Art Café Sienna (daytime), Rozengrāls (book), Folkklubs Ala Pagrabs, 3 Pavāru, or Milda for modern Latvian.
days.backup: Rain: the cellars and Art Nouveau interiors (Riga Art Nouveau Museum on Alberta iela) are the play.
maps: House of the Black Heads Riga

days.theme: Pick up the car at noon. One or two countryside stops — not a marathon.
days.morning: Slow Riga morning. Taxi to Riga Airport (RIX) for the 12:00 PM car pickup. Inspect the car, photograph any damage, confirm cross-border + automatic in writing.
days.afternoon: Drive into the Gauja valley: Turaida Castle (the red-brick tower over the river) and Sigulda. Cēsis only if time allows — don't force it from a noon start.
days.evening: Back to Riga and the Grand Palace for your final Riga night. Park per the hotel's guidance.
days.reservation: Car pickup confirmed (B41497753). Grand Palace night 3.
days.stumble: Turaida and the Gauja overlooks are the 'beauty you stumble into' in countryside form — quiet, green, medieval.
days.food: A simple lunch in Sigulda; dinner back in Riga (Old Town, your pick).
days.backup: If pickup runs late or weather turns, pick ONE stop (Turaida) and keep it relaxed. The car's real job starts tomorrow.

days.transport: Drive Riga → Hill of Crosses → Vilnius (~4.5 hrs + stop)
days.theme: The big countryside transfer. One iconic stop on the way.
days.morning: Check out of the Grand Palace. Drive south toward the Lithuanian border.
days.afternoon: Hill of Crosses near Šiauliai — thousands of crosses on a low hill, eerie and moving. ~30–45 min there, then on to Vilnius.
days.evening: Arrive Vilnius, check into Hotel PACAI, drop bags. Easy first wander: Pilies Street and the courtyards off Didžioji.
days.stumble: Vilnius hides its magic behind archways — push open the courtyard gates off Pilies and Didžioji and see what's inside.
days.food: Easy first-night dinner near PACAI — Lokys (game, medieval cellar) or a Pilies Street spot. Coffee at Eskedar or Italala.
days.backup: If the drive runs long, skip any extra stop and make Hill of Crosses the single one.

days.theme: Island castle by day, hidden Vilnius by evening. Decide the car return.
days.morning: Drive to Trakai early or late to dodge crowds — the red-brick castle on its island in Lake Galvė is the picture-postcard of the trip.
days.afternoon: Back to Vilnius. Užupis (the self-declared artists' republic), the courtyards off Pilies, Cathedral Square.
days.evening: Final dinner — Džiaugsmas, Ertlio Namas, or Grey. Consider Grand Hotel Vilnius afternoon tea earlier if it fits. DECIDE: return the car tonight at VNO or tomorrow at dawn.
days.reservation: Decide car-return timing. Pack for the early flight tonight.
days.stumble: Užupis is the stumble zone — cross the little bridge, find the constitution on the wall, the galleries, the river benches.
days.food: Trakai: try kibinai (the local pastry) lakeside. Vilnius dinner: Džiaugsmas / Ertlio Namas / Lokys / Grey.
days.backup: Rain at Trakai: the castle interior is a museum; or skip Trakai and go deep on Vilnius courtyards and Užupis.

days.theme: Very early departure. Smooth and unhurried beats fast.
days.morning: Airport ~5:30 AM. Return the car at VNO (if not done last night), or taxi over. VNO → AMS departs 7:50 AM.
days.afternoon: Amsterdam connection (KL6027, dep 10:50 AM) → Salt Lake City, arrive 1:08 PM.
days.reservation: Flight XLOIRG · 1 × 23 kg checked bag each · passports.
days.food: Coffee and something small at VNO; the long way home has meals aboard.
days.backup: Build in margin: if anything is unsure about the car return, returning Jul 18 evening + a pre-booked taxi removes all dawn risk.

transport.notes: ~2-hour crossing. Book a departure that allows a relaxed Lilla Roberts checkout and a comfortable Telegraaf check-in. Bring your booking on your phone.
~4.5 hrs. Comfortable coaches (wifi, hot drinks). Easier than the train on this route. Private transfer is the alternative if you'd rather not bus.
Taxi from Grand Palace to RIX. Inspect & photograph the car; confirm cross-border + automatic in writing.
Countryside day trip without changing hotels. Back to Grand Palace for night 3.
Main transfer. ~4.5 hrs driving + the Hill of Crosses stop. Arrive Vilnius before evening.
Trakai is a ~30-min drive. Decide whether to return the car this evening at VNO.
Flight departs 7:50 AM. Return car at VNO very early, or returned Jul 18 evening + taxi.

places.name: Savotta
places.why: Rustic, theatrical Finnish food by the cathedral — cozy and characterful.

places.name: Shelter
places.why: Intimate neighborhood dining, warm room.

places.name: Old Market Hall (Vanha Kauppahalli)
places.why: 1889 market hall — coffee, salmon soup, a graze among stalls.

places.name: Löyly
places.why: Architectural seaside sauna + restaurant — very Finnish, very atmospheric.
places.notes: A bit out from center; book sauna ahead.

places.name: Krog Roba (Hotel Lilla Roberts)
places.why: Your hotel's own room — easiest possible jet-lagged dinner.

places.name: Rataskaevu 16
places.why: Beloved modern Estonian in a candlelit room — the Old Town dinner.

places.name: Olde Hansa
places.why: Full medieval theatre — candlelight, costumes, bear and elk. Lean into the theme.

places.name: Lore Bistroo
places.why: Stylish Kalamaja bistro — the modern contrast to Old Town.

places.name: Fotografiska rooftop
places.why: Rooftop restaurant atop the photography museum — views + drinks.

places.name: Café Maiasmokk
places.why: Tallinn's oldest café (1864) — marzipan, old-world room. A must-coffee.

places.name: Pierre Chocolaterie
places.why: Hidden in the Master's Courtyard — a tiny candlelit chocolate room.

places.name: Kehrwieder (Saiakang)
places.why: Cozy Town Hall Square café down a tiny lane — armchairs and cocoa.

places.name: Kofeman
places.why: Good coffee stop while wandering.

places.name: Loulou
places.why: Pretty breakfast spot for a slow Old Town morning.

places.name: Schlössle Hotel fireplace lounge
places.why: The alternative hotel once considered — drop in as a non-guest for the fireplace room.

places.name: Hotel Telegraaf bar
places.why: Your own hotel's bar — the easiest nightcap room.

places.name: Rozengrāls
places.why: A genuine medieval cellar lit only by candles — the one Riga dinner to reserve.

places.name: Riga Black Magic
places.why: Dark, theatrical bar built around Riga Black Balsam — pure atmosphere.
places.notes: Try the balsam; also a chocolate shop.

places.name: Art Café Sienna
places.why: Coffee among Art Nouveau period rooms in the Alberta iela district.

places.name: Grand Palace Hotel bar
places.why: Your own grand-hotel bar — old-world nightcap.

places.name: Milda
places.why: Modern Latvian, warm and personal.

places.name: 3 Pavāru Restorāns
places.why: '3 Chefs' — inventive tasting cooking, a step up.

places.name: Folkklubs Ala Pagrabs
places.why: Cavernous folk-music cellar — local beer, hearty plates, live music.

places.name: Barents
places.why: If you want a splurge — refined seafood-forward room.

places.name: Džiaugsmas
places.why: Refined modern Lithuanian — a standout final-dinner candidate.

places.name: Ertlio Namas
places.why: Historic Lithuanian recipes in an old townhouse — atmospheric and rooted.

places.name: Lokys
places.why: Game restaurant in a 15th-century cellar — beaver, boar, candle glow.

places.name: Grey
places.why: Contemporary tasting room for a modern contrast.

places.name: Grand Hotel Vilnius afternoon tea
places.why: Grand-hotel afternoon tea — only if it fits the day.

places.name: Eskedar Coffee Bar
places.why: Lovely specialty coffee stop while wandering Old Town.

places.name: Italala Caffe
places.why: Cozy café for a courtyard pause.

places.name: Augustas & Barbora Love Story Café
places.why: A romantic, story-themed café — fitting for the two of you.

places.name: Užupis cafés & wine bars
places.why: The artists' republic across the river — riverside wine and benches.

places.name: Nineteen18 (Hotel PACAI)
places.why: Your hotel's own restaurant — convenient first or last night.

castles.name: Turaida Castle
castles.why: A restored red-brick medieval castle with a tower over the Gauja river — the classic Latvian countryside picture.
castles.parking: Paid lot at the museum reserve.
castles.photo: Climb the main tower for the river-valley view.

castles.name: Sigulda
castles.why: Spa-town gateway to the Gauja valley — overlooks, a cable car across the valley, easy strolls.

castles.name: Gauja National Park
castles.why: Latvia's largest national park — forested river valley, sandstone cliffs, castle ruins.

castles.name: Cēsis
castles.why: A pretty medieval town with castle ruins and candle-lit tower tours.
castles.rec: OPTIONAL — skip if starting at noon.

castles.name: Hill of Crosses
castles.why: Thousands upon thousands of crosses on a low hill — strange, solemn, unforgettable. The one iconic stop between Riga and Vilnius.
castles.photo: Wide shot of the cross-covered mounds.

castles.name: Trakai Castle
castles.why: A storybook red-brick castle on its own island in a lake — Lithuania's most photographed sight.
castles.timing: Jul 18, early or late for fewer crowds
castles.photo: The castle across the water from the footbridge.
castles.rec: KEEP — the Vilnius day trip. Try kibinai lakeside.

budget.note: Cost not on screenshot; Michael estimated ≈ $500–600. PLUS fuel + one-way/cross-border fees.

budget_unknowns: Grace's flight ticket (likely similar to Michael's ≈ USD 1,597 if separately issued).
Rental car final total + one-way drop fee + cross-border fee + fuel.
Ferry (Helsinki→Tallinn) and bus (Tallinn→Riga) fares — both unbooked.
Meals, museum entries, taxis, and incidentals across 11 days.
Parking in Riga and Vilnius Old Towns.
